task_completion.description: Verify that a survey titled 'TechSummit 2024 Post-Event Feedback' has been created and activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the survey title 'TechSummit 2024 Post-Event Feedback' in the LimeSurvey header or survey list. The survey must be active, which is typically indicated by an 'Active' status badge, a green checkmark, or a 'Stop this survey' button in the top toolbar.

task_completion.description: Verify that the five required question groups have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the survey structure panel (usually on the left) or the group list. It should display the following five groups: 'Attendance Information', 'Venue and Logistics', 'Virtual Experience', 'Overall Satisfaction', and 'Follow-up'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'AttendType' question in the 'Attendance Information' group.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a question with the code 'AttendType'. It must be marked as mandatory (often indicated by a red asterisk) and be a 'List (Radio)' type question. Its answer options must include In-person (code A1), Virtual (code A2), and Hybrid (code A3).

task_completion.description: Verify the conditional logic (relevance equations) for the 'Venue and Logistics' and 'Virtual Experience' groups.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the group settings or the 'Survey logic file' view. The relevance equation for 'Venue and Logistics' should check if AttendType is A1 or A3 (e.g., `AttendType == 'A1' or AttendType == 'A3'`). The relevance equation for 'Virtual Experience' should check if AttendType is A2 or A3 (e.g., `AttendType == 'A2' or AttendType == 'A3'`).

task_completion.description: Verify the questions in the 'Overall Satisfaction' group.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a mandatory 5-point choice question with the code 'QOverall' and a Yes/No question (e.g., 'Recommendation') within the 'Overall Satisfaction' group in the survey structure or question list.

task_completion.description: Verify the conditional logic (relevance equations) for the 'Testimonial' and 'Issues' questions in the 'Follow-up' group.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the question settings or the 'Survey logic file' view. The 'Testimonial' question must have a relevance equation checking if QOverall is 4 or higher (e.g., `QOverall >= 4`). The 'Issues' question must have a relevance equation checking if QOverall is 2 or lower (e.g., `QOverall <= 2`).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the LimeSurvey web interface to create the survey.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LimeSurvey admin dashboard and form editors, not terminal windows executing SQL commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Elements, Console, etc.) should not be open in the screenshots.

integrity.description: Verify that the relevance equations are actually entered into the LimeSurvey interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The relevance equations should be visible in the 'Relevance equation' text boxes in group/question settings, or in the structured 'Survey logic file' table, not just typed into a separate text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: LimeSurvey 6.x uses 'Relevance equations' (ExpressionScript) for conditional logic. Group-level logic is configured in the group settings, and question-level logic in the question settings. The 'Survey logic file' or 'Check logic' tool displays all relevance equations in a tabular format. 'List (Radio)' is the standard question type for single-choice questions with custom codes (like A1, A2). A 5-point choice question and Yes/No question are specific question types in LimeSurvey. Activation status is shown in the top toolbar or survey list.
